basics.name: LEE MULLINS
basics.label: Data Engineer & Biomedical Informatics Specialist
basics.summary: Data engineer and biomedical informatics specialist focused on building observable, containerized data pipelines for medical imaging. Experienced in QC gating logic, high-integrity data transformations, and HIPAA/IRB-compliant workflows. Passionate about reproducible research and cross-functional collaboration in complex data environments.

work.name: Emory University School of Medicine
highlights: Developed reproducible pipelines for MRI data, enabling scalable infant health research.
Created tools for structural MRI QC (bias correction, skull stripping), packaging workflows for team-wide reuse.
Converted DICOM to BIDS with strict metadata enforcement and PHI scrubbing; ensured NIH/NDA audit readiness.
Standardized cross-lab datasets by implementing clear directory structures, automated CSV generation, and permissioned access to reduce confusion and data loss.
Bridged communication gaps across researchers, postdocs, and IT by writing documentation, training users, and managing updates.
Advocated for better workflow planning, building systems to track dataset progress and support cross-functional collaboration.
Introductory talk for parents and community members on how AI works, what it can and can't do, and its growing role in education, work, and daily life.
Led an interactive talk on proteins, genomics, brain imaging, and AI, including a demo on how gene mutations affect phenotype and a Q&A on future tech and medicine.

projects.name: Salesforce Agentforce Hackathon
projects.summary: Designing a GenAI-powered agent to monitor CRM data quality, detect anomalies, and suggest standardizations.
highlights: Uses approval-based automation to maintain governance and trust.

projects.name: BIDS MRI QC Pipeline
projects.summary: Built a modular QC pipeline for structural/functional MRI in BIDS format.
highlights: Included automated preprocessing, a web-based annotation tool, gated validation, and HTML report generation.

projects.name: DICOM-to-BIDS Conversion Package
projects.summary: Developed a pip-installable tool to convert raw DICOM to BIDS-compliant datasets.
highlights: PHI scrubbing, JSON validation, and structured logging.

projects.name: Cell Classification Pipeline on AWS
projects.summary: Deployed a secure cloud-based inference pipeline for single-cell classification.
highlights: Enabled OAuth2-authenticated access and logged results to DynamoDB for scalable team use.
